summary: A two-story residential building with a gable roof made of brick. The structure features a sandstone/stone block main body with regular window openings on both floors, a white concrete foundation/platform base, and a distinctive brick chimney or tower element on top. The building is symmetrical and compact, sitting on a dirt foundation surrounded by grass.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with gable roof and central chimney/tower

elements: windows (4 per floor, arranged in 2x2 grid pattern)
rebuild_hints: Build 8x8 footprint foundation platform using concrete blocks
Construct main walls using sandstone blocks, 2 blocks tall per floor
Place 4 window openings per floor in symmetrical 2x2 arrangement
Add gable roof using brick blocks, sloped on both sides
Build central brick chimney/tower structure on roof peak
Add white concrete trim around foundation perimeter
Ensure bilateral symmetry across all axes
Windows appear to be 1x1 or 1x2 glass panes with frame
uncertainties: Exact window dimensions and frame material (appears to be glass with possible wood or stone frame)
Interior layout and room configuration not visible
Precise height of gable roof peak (estimated 10 blocks total)
Whether chimney/tower is functional or decorative
Exact sandstone variant (regular vs chiseled vs cut)
Door location not clearly visible in any view
